title: Weekly Academic Update: Health Programmes and Family Planning
summary: 13 section(s) were revised under Chapter 7 (Health Programmes in India) and Chapter 8 (Demography and Family Planning). Key revised topics: Contraceptive Methods Part 1: Spacing Methods and Intra-Uterine Devices, Contraceptive Methods Part 2: Hormonal and Non-Hormonal Methods, Demographic Indicators, Surveys, and Registration Systems, Demography Basics and Demographic Trends, Evaluation, Unmet Need, Delivery System, and New Initiatives, Family Planning, National Population Policy, and Fertility Indicators, +7 more. This update is intended for MD Community Medicine learners for rapid revision of policy- and programme-linked content.
source: Automated weekly verification pipeline using current public-health guidance available at verification time.

title: TRAI Assesses Network Quality at Key Puducherry Medical Facilities
summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) assessed mobile network quality at key healthcare facilities in Puducherry, including JIPMER Medical College and Rajiv Gandhi Government Women and Children Hospital, during its February 2026 independent drive test. Findings covered voice and data services from Airtel, BSNL, RJIL, and VIL, crucial for operational efficiency and emergency communications. This assessment highlights the importance of reliable telecom infrastructure for supporting public health services in the region.

title: India Proposes Drugs Rules Amendment to Streamline Blood Product Viral Testing
summary: India's MoHFW seeks public comments on amending the Drugs Rules, 1945, to streamline blood product viral testing. The proposed change aims to remove duplicate testing of finished products, as pooled plasma is already rigorously screened for HIV, Hepatitis B, and C as per global pharmacopoeial standards. This aligns regulations, rationalizes testing, reduces compliance burden, and upholds patient safety.

title: India Begins 100-Day Countdown to International Yoga Day 2026
summary: The Ministry of Ayush and Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga are organizing Yoga Mahotsav-2026 on March 13, 2026, marking the 100-day countdown to International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2026. This initiative involves 100 organizations promoting yoga in 100 cities. The program aims to expand outreach, encourage public participation, and prepare for the 12th IDY, scheduled for commemoration at 100 iconic locations, inspiring holistic well-being through yoga. Preparations are gaining momentum.
